name: Moroccan Chicken Tagine
tagline: Slow-braised chicken with preserved lemon, olives, saffron, ginger
highlight: No soy, no nuts (just olives) — Morocco's iconic Friday dish

hero.alt: Chicken tagine with preserved lemon and green olives
hero.caption: tagine — preserved lemon and olive

healthFacts: Preserved lemon undergoes lacto-fermentation — flavors deepen, vitamin C drops, but probiotics develop.
Saffron's crocin is a fat-soluble carotenoid — a little goes far.

steps.title: Marinate briefly
steps.detail: Pat chicken dry. Rub with cumin, coriander, ginger, garlic paste, salt, olive oil. Rest 20 min.
steps.tip: Even 20 min lets the spices wake up; longer never hurts.

steps.title: Brown chicken
steps.detail: In a tagine or Dutch oven, brown chicken skin-down in olive oil 6 min. Flip 3 min. Set aside.
steps.tip: Skin-down first sets up the schmaltz that flavors the whole pot.

steps.title: Soften onion, bloom saffron
steps.detail: Sweat onion 8 min in chicken fat. Bloom saffron in 1/2 cup hot water; pour into pot with onion.
steps.tip: Saffron in cold liquid fades; hot water unlocks its color.

steps.title: Slow braise
steps.detail: Return chicken with juices. Tuck olives and preserved lemon around. Cover, simmer low 35 min until chicken slips off the bone. Shower with herbs.
steps.tip: Couscous is the classic side — soaks up that golden sauce.
